Model K Comparison Dashboard

High Volatility Extreme vs Total Market

This page compares the baseline Model_K.py evaluation across every scored BTC hourly market against the High Volatility Extreme slice from Model_K_Volatility_Decomposition_RT.py. The volatility slice is defined as: Hourly realized volatility at or above the trailing 72-hour rolling 90th percentile.

High Volatility Extreme hourly markets: 154 Total hourly markets: 1,268 Segment share of markets: 0.1215 Outcome match rate: 1.0000 vs 1.0000

Topline Comparison

metric volatility total delta
Scored rows 27,359 226,149 -198,790
Event contracts 462 3,804 -3,342
Brier score 0.1539 0.1269 +0.0270
Log loss 0.4546 0.3886 +0.0659
ECE 0.0449 0.0107 +0.0342

The total-market baseline includes the volatility segment itself. This dashboard is showing how the subset behaves relative to the full scored sample, not a disjoint holdout.

These audit mismatches are diagnostic only. They compare official Kalshi settlement results against the Binance strike audit already used by the existing scripts and are not scored as truth.

Volatility Threshold Context

threshold_name percentile window_hours latest_realized_volatility_cutoff median_realized_volatility_cutoff min_realized_volatility_cutoff max_realized_volatility_cutoff
Low Volatility Extreme 0.1000 72 0.0025 0.0026 0.0013 0.0048
Low Volatility 0.2500 72 0.0030 0.0030 0.0015 0.0055
High Volatility 0.7500 72 0.0049 0.0053 0.0027 0.0084
High Volatility Extreme 0.9000 72 0.0063 0.0069 0.0033 0.0111

The real-time volatility segmentation uses a trailing 72-hour window, equivalent to 4320 Binance one-minute observations.

Assumptions

  • Kalshi prices are YES probabilities. CSV values greater than 1 and no greater than 100 are treated as cents and divided by 100 before scoring.
  • Scored event outcome is the official Kalshi contract result: 1 for YES resolved, 0 for NO resolved.
  • Forecast rows are joined to outcomes by exact Kalshi market ticker when available; legacy price files without market tickers fall back to event_ticker + contract_label + strike.
  • Event tickers are interpreted as the contract settlement/end hour, matching the price fetcher's hour_end_utc -> New York event ticker convention.
  • Binance prices are diagnostic only. They may be used for strike matching upstream and audit mismatch checks, but never as the scored truth label.
  • Events present in the Kalshi price files but absent from the settlement CSV are excluded from scoring and counted in outcome_join_coverage.csv.
  • Volatility decomposition uses Binance BTCUSDT 1-minute candles aligned to each scored Kalshi forecast hour.
  • Hourly realized volatility is defined as sqrt(sum(log(close/open)^2)) across the 60 one-minute Binance bars inside the forecast hour.
  • Real-time classification uses the trailing 72-hour (4320-minute) Binance window available at each scored hour.
  • Rolling cutoff summaries in this run: q10 latest=0.00251833, median=0.00256496, range=[0.00129308, 0.00479875]; q25 latest=0.00302169, median=0.00304949, range=[0.00151308, 0.00547011]; q75 latest=0.00487723, median=0.00529946, range=[0.00265512, 0.00838105]; q90 latest=0.00632642, median=0.00692337, range=[0.00328579, 0.01111803].
  • Low Volatility includes hours with realized volatility <= trailing q25; Standard Volatility includes trailing q25 < realized volatility < trailing q75; High Volatility includes realized volatility >= trailing q75.

Run Notes

  • Loaded official Kalshi contract outcomes from kalshi_btc_atm_settlements.csv.
  • Reused hourly volatility segment table from /Users/socks/Repositories/Binary-Options-Market-Volatility-Research/Model_K_Volatility_Decomposition_RT/Model_K_Volatility_Decomposition_RT_outputs/hourly_market_volatility_segments.csv.
  • Dashboard inputs come from Data_Sourcing/Kalshi_Pricing_Fetch and Data_Sourcing/Settlement_Outcomes, matching the existing scripts.
  • This formatter keeps the same Model K scoring, calibration, Brier decomposition, and time-bucket logic. Only the presentation layer is new.

Back to dashboard index
